text: Max pooling is a technique used in neural networks to reduce the spatial dimensions of feature maps. Let's see how it works.
Here we have a four by four input feature map containing sixteen values.
We divide the map into four non-overlapping two by two windows. In each window, we select the maximum value.
The maximum values are highlighted in different colors: six, eight, seven, and nine.
These maximum values form our output feature map, reducing the spatial size from four by four to two by two.
Max pooling effectively downsamples the feature map while preserving the most salient features.
